changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_logging_configured for clarity
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's focus on continuous monitoring. However, minor naming adjustments were made for clarity and consistency. The function names now better reflect the specific actions being monitored or configured.
naming_improvements: Use 'configured' instead of 'enabled' for audit logging to indicate setup completeness
Use 'logged' instead of 'monitoring_enabled' for network policy changes to specify the action
title: Continuous Monitoring Strategy - Ongoing monitoring of organizationally-defined metrics...
step1_reasoning: The control PM-31-c focuses on continuous monitoring of organizationally-defined metrics, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through audit logging and monitoring of network policy changes. Kubernetes audit logs can be configured to capture API server access and other critical events, similar to cloud logging services. Additionally, monitoring changes to network policies can be achieved through logging and alerting mechanisms. These mappings ensure ongoing visibility and monitoring within a Kubernetes environment.
